basics.name: Yin Wu
basics.summary: Ph.D. researcher in ADAS/AD verification and validation, with experience in scenario generation, scenario mining, multi-sensor perception, reinforcement learning planners, and ADAS system integration.

work.position: Ph.D. Student, Scenario and Testing Department
work.summary: Research and project work in data-driven ADAS/AD verification and validation.
highlights: Contributed to PoCs for an LLM-based scenario agentic editor and LLM-based test case generation.
Supported corner case detection from bus data.

work.summary: Developed an online HD map generation network using LiDAR point clouds.
highlights: Built and maintained GitLab CI/CD pipelines for production deployment.

work.summary: Fusion of Multi-Information to Improve Object Detection Results for LiDAR Point Clouds.
highlights: Developed a sensor fusion and object tracking system for autonomous driving.

work.summary: Assembled and integrated edge-computing LiDAR systems.
highlights: Deployed hardware solutions at customer sites.
Developed evaluation software for LiDAR system performance.

work.summary: Built a multi-sensor data collection system with LiDAR, cameras, and GNSS/IMU sensors.
highlights: Worked on sensor calibration and time synchronization.

work.summary: Tutored exercise classes and provided MATLAB programming guidance.

education.area: Data-driven Verification and Validation in ADAS/AD
courses: Virtual scenario generation for ODD coverage

publications.name: Efficient Cross-Country Data Acquisition Strategy for ADAS via Street-View Imagery
publications.summary: Leveraged street-view imagery to identify out-of-distribution traffic elements for efficient ADAS test route planning.

publications.name: Why Braking? Scenario Extraction and Reasoning Utilizing LLM
publications.summary: Integrated LLMs into a rule-based framework for scenario extraction, enabling both rule-based and semantic scenario search.

publications.name: LanePerf: a Performance Estimation Framework for Lane Detection
publications.summary: Developed a lane detection performance estimator using features from foundation models and ADAS perception models.
